executive_summary.lead: Johndup is a standalone Deposit Money Bank operating in a single state with a customer base under 10,000, processing 1,000–50,000 daily transactions. The institution is rated Medium risk by the CBN and maintains PEP exposure through its retail deposit and trade finance operations.
body_paragraphs: CBN Circular BSD/DIR/PUB/LAB/019/002, issued March 10, 2026, mandates that all DMBs deploy automated AML/CFT/CPF solutions within 18 months. Manual controls are no longer acceptable. The circular requires real-time transaction monitoring, AI-driven anomaly detection, and full integration between AML systems and core banking platforms. A 12-section Implementation Roadmap must be submitted by June 10, 2026.

Johndup currently operates entirely manual AML processes across all functional areas including CDD/KYC, sanctions screening, transaction monitoring, fraud detection, case management, and regulatory reporting. Zero of twelve Baseline Standards are compliant. Governance score is 0/10 — no MLRO, no board-approved policy, no BVN/NIN integration, no internal audit coverage of AML. All 12 standards constitute Critical Gaps.

Johndup faces a hard June 10, 2026 deadline for roadmap submission — 26 days from this assessment. Non-submission constitutes regulatory non-compliance. OPEX Consulting can complete the CBN template using these findings and coordinate vendor selection, policy drafting, and phased implementation to meet the September 2027 compliance deadline.
executive_summary.inline_alert: CBN Circular BSD/DIR/PUB/LAB/019/002 states: 'Manual AML controls are no longer sufficient. All DMBs must deploy automated solutions supporting real-time monitoring, AI/ML anomaly detection, and full integration with core banking systems within 18 months.'

overall_rating.sector_context_note: Johndup operates entirely manual AML processes. Zero governance controls are in place. All 12 CBN Baseline Standards constitute Critical Gaps. Immediate executive intervention required.

scorecard.regulatory_context_box: Johndup has seen vendor demos but has not started roadmap preparation. Budget constraints are the primary concern. No recent CBN examination activity reported.

profile.sector_context_box: As a DMB, Johndup faces the strictest compliance timeline — 18 months to full compliance. Trade finance and PEP exposure elevate sanctions screening requirements under 5.3.

requirement_categories_intro: The CBN Baseline Standards apply differentially based on institution type, risk classification, and product profile. Johndup's status as a DMB with PEP exposure triggers all mandatory requirements plus conditional obligations for enhanced sanctions screening and trade finance monitoring.
requirement_categories_alert: Johndup must address all 12 Baseline Standards. As a DMB, standalone transaction feeds are prohibited under 5.10(d). Full integration with core banking and KYC systems is mandatory.

requirement_categories.area: Integrated AML Solution — core functional footprint
requirement_categories.trigger: All DMBs must deploy automated solutions supporting 9 functional areas including CDD, sanctions, monitoring, reporting, and audit.

requirement_categories.area: CDD, KYC and KYB — real-time identity corroboration
requirement_categories.trigger: All institutions must integrate BVN/NIN for real-time identity verification. Manual batch processes are non-compliant.

requirement_categories.area: Sanctions and PEP Screening — real-time interdiction
requirement_categories.trigger: All institutions must screen against domestic and international lists with automated transaction hold on confirmed matches.

requirement_categories.area: Sanctions Screening — enhanced coverage for PEP exposure
requirement_categories.trigger: Johndup's PEP exposure requires screening against OFAC, UN, EU, and UK HMT lists beyond CBN/EFCC domestic coverage.

requirement_categories.area: Customer Risk Assessment — dynamic scoring with AI governance
requirement_categories.trigger: All institutions must implement dynamic risk scoring. AI/ML models require documented governance framework with human oversight.
DMBs must deploy real-time monitoring across payment channels. Manual review of transaction logs is non-compliant.

requirement_categories.area: Transaction Monitoring — automated alert closure criteria
requirement_categories.trigger: Automated closure requires governance approval, KYC/transactional context, low customer risk, and no unresolved alerts.

requirement_categories.area: Transaction Monitoring — documented Alert Review SLAs
requirement_categories.trigger: All institutions must document and enforce SLAs for alert investigation and escalation.
All institutions must deploy real-time fraud monitoring. Johndup currently has no fraud detection capability.

requirement_categories.area: Case Management — Enterprise Case Management with audit trails
requirement_categories.trigger: All institutions must implement ECM with Maker-Checker workflows and immutable audit logs.
All institutions must automate regulatory filing. Manual goAML portal submission does not satisfy 5.8 automation requirements.

requirement_categories.area: Audit and Governance — immutable audit trails
requirement_categories.trigger: All institutions must maintain tamper-proof logs of all AML system activity for regulatory examination.

requirement_categories.area: System Integration and Scalability — prohibition on standalone feeds
requirement_categories.trigger: DMBs are prohibited from using standalone transaction feeds. Full bidirectional integration with core banking is mandatory.

requirement_categories.area: Security and Data Protection — encryption and MFA
requirement_categories.trigger: All institutions must implement encryption at rest/transit and enforce MFA. Johndup currently has neither.

requirement_categories.area: Security and Data Protection — Business Impact Analysis
requirement_categories.trigger: All institutions must conduct BIA with defined RTO/RPO for AML systems. Johndup has no BIA.

requirement_categories.area: User Interface and Customisation — real-time dashboards
requirement_categories.trigger: All institutions must provide real-time AML metrics dashboards for compliance officers and investigators.
All institutions must maintain board-approved AML policy. Johndup has no documented policy.
All institutions must document vendor management frameworks for AML solution providers.
All institutions must submit 12-section roadmap by June 10, 2026. Incomplete submissions constitute non-compliance.

gap_analysis_intro: Johndup operates entirely manual AML processes across all functional areas. The following analysis maps each of the 12 CBN Baseline Standards to specific gaps, required actions, and RegTech365 solutions. All 12 standards constitute Critical Gaps requiring immediate remediation.

standards.title: Integrated AML Solution
standards.finding: Johndup has no automated AML platform. Standard 5.1(a) requires integrated solutions supporting CDD/KYC, sanctions screening, risk assessment, transaction monitoring, fraud detection, case management, reporting, audit trails, and data protection. Manual processes across all areas constitute non-compliance.
standards.required_action: Deploy integrated AML platform supporting all 9 functional areas before September 2027 compliance deadline. Initiate vendor selection immediately to allow 18-month implementation runway.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort provides the core integrated platform addressing CDD/KYC, sanctions, transaction monitoring, and regulatory reporting with purpose-built NIBSS/goAML integration for Nigerian DMBs.

standards.title: CDD, KYC and KYB
standards.finding: Johndup conducts manual KYC with batch BVN lookups. Standard 5.2(b)(i) mandates real-time identity corroboration via BVN/NIN linkage at account opening and periodic refresh. Manual processes cannot satisfy real-time requirements. UBO mapping is manual, creating trade finance compliance risk.
standards.required_action: Implement real-time BVN/NIN integration via NIBSS APIs. Automate UBO mapping for corporate accounts. Deploy dynamic KYC refresh triggers based on risk score changes.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort integrates directly with NIBSS for real-time BVN/NIN verification, automated UBO mapping, and dynamic KYC refresh workflows triggered by risk score escalation or transaction anomalies.

standards.title: Sanctions and PEP Screening
standards.finding: Johndup screens manually against CBN/EFCC lists only. Standard 5.3(a)(viii) requires real-time screening with automated transaction interdiction on confirmed matches. PEP exposure triggers enhanced coverage requirements — OFAC, UN, EU, UK HMT lists are mandatory. Fuzzy matching and name variant detection are absent.
standards.required_action: Deploy real-time sanctions screening with fuzzy matching across domestic and international lists. Implement automated transaction hold on confirmed matches. Expand coverage to OFAC, UN, EU, UK HMT.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort delivers real-time AI-driven sanctions screening with fuzzy matching, name variant detection, and automated interdiction. Pre-configured list coverage includes CBN/EFCC, OFAC, UN, EU, UK HMT with daily updates.

standards.title: Customer Risk Assessment
standards.finding: Johndup conducts manual risk assessments at onboarding with no dynamic refresh. Standard 5.4(a)(iv) requires automated risk scoring that updates based on transactional behavior, KYC changes, and external triggers. AI/ML models require documented governance framework with human oversight and explainability — currently absent.
standards.required_action: Implement dynamic risk scoring engine with automated refresh triggers. Establish Model Governance Committee to oversee AI/ML risk models. Document explainability and human oversight protocols.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort provides dynamic risk scoring with automated refresh based on transaction patterns, KYC updates, and sanctions hits. RegComply delivers the governance framework for model oversight and audit trail documentation.

standards.title: Transaction Monitoring and Risk-Based Analyses
standards.finding: Johndup reviews transaction logs manually. Standard 5.5(b)(i) mandates real-time multi-scenario monitoring across all payment channels. Automated alert closure is prohibited unless 4 strict criteria are met: governance approval, KYC/transactional context, low customer risk, no unresolved alerts. Johndup has no documented Alert Review SLAs, violating 5.5(b)(vi).
standards.required_action: Deploy real-time transaction monitoring with multi-scenario rule engine. Document and enforce Alert Review SLAs. Establish governance-approved criteria for automated low-risk alert closure.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort delivers real-time transaction monitoring with configurable scenario library and network analysis. RegComply enforces documented Alert Review SLAs and governance-approved automated closure criteria with full audit trails.

standards.title: Fraud Monitoring and Detection
standards.finding: Johndup has no fraud monitoring capability. Standard 5.6(a) requires real-time fraud detection across digital channels. As a DMB, Johndup must roadmap toward a Unified Financial Crime Risk Architecture per 5.6(b)(i), where fraud signals feed into AML risk scoring. Current manual processes create regulatory and operational risk.
standards.required_action: Deploy real-time fraud monitoring across ATM/POS channels. Establish fraud-to-AML risk feed to create unified financial crime architecture. Document roadmap for full integration.
standards.regtech_solution: RegGuard provides real-time fraud monitoring with behavioral analytics and device fingerprinting. Fraud signals feed directly into RegPort's risk scoring engine, creating the unified architecture required by 5.6(b)(i).

standards.title: Case Management
standards.finding: Johndup manages alerts and investigations manually with informal approval processes. Standard 5.7(a) requires Enterprise Case Management with Maker-Checker workflows, immutable audit trails, and structured investigation documentation. Manual case files cannot satisfy examination requirements for tamper-proof evidence chains.
standards.required_action: Implement ECM with Maker-Checker workflows for alert escalation, investigation, and STR filing. Ensure immutable audit trails for all case activity.
standards.regtech_solution: RegComply delivers Enterprise Case Management with enforced Maker-Checker workflows, immutable audit logs, and structured investigation templates. All case activity is timestamped and tamper-proof for CBN examination.

standards.title: Regulatory Reporting
standards.finding: Johndup files STRs manually via goAML portal with informal approval. Standard 5.8(a) requires automated generation of STR, SAR, CTR, and FTR with structured data validation and audit trails. Manual portal submission does not satisfy automation requirements. Informal approval violates documented workflow requirements.
standards.required_action: Automate regulatory report generation with structured data validation. Implement formal Maker-Checker approval workflows. Integrate with goAML for automated submission.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort automates STR/SAR/CTR/FTR generation with pre-validation against goAML schema requirements. RegComply enforces Maker-Checker approval workflows with full audit trails before automated submission.

standards.title: Audit and Governance
standards.finding: Johndup maintains manual audit logs with no tamper-proof controls. Standard 5.9(a) requires immutable audit trails for all AML system activity. Internal audit does not cover AML, violating governance requirements. If AI/ML is deployed, 5.5(b)(i) mandates annual independent validation — currently no framework exists.
standards.required_action: Implement tamper-proof audit logging for all AML activity. Establish internal audit coverage of AML with quarterly reviews. Prepare AI/ML validation framework for future deployment.
standards.regtech_solution: RegComply provides immutable audit trails with cryptographic timestamping for all system activity. Audit reports are auto-generated for internal audit and CBN examination. AI/ML validation framework is built-in.

standards.title: System Integration and Scalability
standards.finding: Johndup has no AML system to integrate. Standard 5.10(d) explicitly prohibits standalone transaction feeds for DMBs. Full bidirectional API integration with core banking and KYC systems is mandatory. Manual data extraction cannot satisfy real-time monitoring requirements under 5.5.
standards.required_action: Deploy AML platform with secure bidirectional API integration to core banking. Ensure real-time transaction feeds and KYC data synchronization. Document integration architecture for CBN examination.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort integrates via secure REST APIs with Nigerian core banking platforms including Finacle, T24, and BankOne. Real-time transaction feeds and KYC synchronization are standard. Integration architecture documentation is auto-generated.

standards.title: Security and Data Protection
standards.finding: Johndup has no encryption or MFA for AML data. Standard 5.11(a) requires encryption at rest and in transit plus enforced MFA. No Business Impact Analysis exists for AML systems, violating 5.11(c) requirements for defined RTO/RPO. Data sovereignty is confirmed (Nigeria), satisfying NDPA requirements.
standards.required_action: Implement AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit. Enforce MFA for all AML system access. Conduct BIA with defined RTO/RPO for AML platform.
standards.regtech_solution: RegPort enforces AES-256 encryption, TLS 1.3, and mandatory MFA. RegComply includes BIA templates with RTO/RPO documentation. All data is hosted in Nigeria, ensuring NDPA compliance.

standards.title: User Interface and Customisation
standards.finding: Johndup has no AML dashboards. Standard 5.12(a) requires real-time metrics for alert volumes, case status, STR filings, and risk distribution. Investigators need efficient navigation tools for case review. Manual spreadsheets cannot satisfy real-time requirements or examination readiness.
standards.required_action: Deploy real-time AML dashboards for compliance officers and investigators. Ensure efficient case navigation and drill-down capabilities. Provide role-based access controls.
standards.regtech_solution: RegComply delivers real-time dashboards with configurable widgets for alert volumes, case aging, STR status, and risk heatmaps. Role-based access ensures investigators see only relevant cases. Drill-down to transaction details is one-click.

governance_assessment.intro: The CBN Baseline Standards require a comprehensive governance framework spanning policy, oversight, and operational controls. Johndup has zero of ten mandatory governance controls in place, creating critical examination risk. The following assessment maps each control to CBN requirements and remediation actions.
governance_assessment.score_context: Zero governance controls are in place. Johndup has no MLRO, no board-approved AML policy, no BVN/NIN integration, no vendor management framework, and no internal audit coverage of AML. This constitutes critical non-compliance. CBN examination would result in immediate regulatory action.

items.action_required: Appoint MLRO with board approval and documented terms of reference before roadmap submission.
Draft and obtain board approval for comprehensive AML/CFT/CPF policy before June 10, 2026.
Establish governance framework covering system ownership, change control, and escalation protocols.

items.control: Formal Change Control for AML Configurations
items.action_required: Implement change control process with Maker-Checker approval for scenario rules and thresholds.
Establish committee before AI/ML deployment to oversee model validation and explainability.
Document SLAs for alert investigation, escalation, and closure before system deployment.
Establish vendor management framework covering due diligence, SLAs, and exit planning.
Document retention periods for AML data and secure destruction protocols.
Implement real-time BVN/NIN integration via NIBSS APIs before September 2027.

items.control: AML Training Programme with Documented Records
items.action_required: Deploy digital training platform with certification tracking for all staff.

priority_actions.title: CBN Implementation Roadmap Submission
priority_actions.deadline_label: HARD DEADLINE: 10 JUNE 2026 · MANDATORY FOR ALL INSTITUTIONS
priority_actions.body:
Johndup must submit the 12-section CBN Implementation Roadmap Template by June 10, 2026 — 26 days from this assessment. The template requires executive summary, gap analysis, implementation strategy, timeline, resource plan, vendor selection status, governance framework, training plan, testing protocols, risk mitigation, budget, and board sign-off. Incomplete submissions constitute non-compliance.

OPEX Consulting can complete this template using these assessment findings, coordinate vendor selection documentation, and prepare board presentation materials. Immediate engagement is required to meet the June 10 deadline.

priority_actions.title: MLRO Appointment and Board AML Policy Approval
priority_actions.body:
Johndup must appoint a formally designated MLRO or CCO with documented terms of reference and board approval. The MLRO must have direct board access and authority over AML operations. Concurrently, the board must approve a comprehensive AML/CFT/CPF policy covering risk appetite, governance structure, escalation protocols, and regulatory reporting obligations.

OPEX Consulting can draft the AML/CFT/CPF policy template, prepare MLRO terms of reference, and coordinate board approval process. These governance foundations are prerequisites for credible roadmap submission.

priority_actions.title: AML Platform Vendor Selection and Contracting
priority_actions.deadline_label: COMPLETE BY AUGUST 2026 · 18-MONTH IMPLEMENTATION RUNWAY REQUIRED
priority_actions.body:
Johndup has seen vendor demos but has not selected a platform. Budget constraints are the primary concern. The 18-month compliance timeline requires vendor selection and contracting by August 2026 to allow adequate implementation runway. Delayed vendor selection compresses implementation timelines and increases delivery risk.

OPEX Consulting can conduct structured vendor evaluation using CBN Baseline Standards as scoring criteria, negotiate commercial terms, and coordinate proof-of-concept testing. RegTech365 offers flexible commercial models including phased deployment to manage budget constraints.

priority_actions.title: BVN/NIN Real-Time Integration via NIBSS
priority_actions.deadline_label: CRITICAL DEPENDENCY FOR 5.2 COMPLIANCE · COMPLETE BY Q1 2027
priority_actions.body:
Johndup conducts manual batch BVN lookups. Standard 5.2(b)(i) mandates real-time identity corroboration at account opening and periodic refresh. NIBSS API integration is the only compliant approach. This is a critical dependency for CDD/KYC compliance and must be completed before transaction monitoring deployment.

RegPort includes pre-built NIBSS integration with real-time BVN/NIN verification, automated UBO mapping, and dynamic KYC refresh workflows. OPEX Consulting can coordinate NIBSS onboarding and API testing.

priority_actions.title: Internal Audit Coverage of AML Systems
priority_actions.deadline_label: ESTABLISH BY Q2 2027 · QUARTERLY REVIEWS REQUIRED
priority_actions.body:
Johndup's internal audit does not cover AML. Standard 5.9 requires independent validation of AML controls including scenario effectiveness, alert quality, and governance compliance. Quarterly internal audit reviews are required to demonstrate ongoing compliance and identify control gaps before CBN examination.

OPEX Consulting offers internal audit co-sourcing for AML systems including scenario testing, alert sampling, and governance framework validation. RegComply auto-generates audit reports with evidence packs for internal audit and CBN examination.

roadmap.intro: The following roadmap spans 28 months from May 2025 to September 2027, structured in four phases aligned to CBN compliance milestones. Phase 1 focuses on governance baseline and roadmap submission. Phase 2 establishes architecture and policy frameworks. Phase 3 deploys core AML systems. Phase 4 ensures examination readiness through audit, training, and evidence pack preparation.

phases.title: Foundation and Roadmap Submission
phases.description: Establish governance baseline and submit CBN Implementation Roadmap by June 10, 2026 deadline. Appoint MLRO with board-approved terms of reference. Draft and obtain board approval for AML/CFT/CPF policy. Conduct vendor evaluation and select AML platform provider. Document current-state gap analysis and implementation strategy. Prepare 12-section roadmap template with executive summary, timeline, resource plan, and board sign-off.
deliverables: MLRO appointment with board-approved terms of reference
CBN Implementation Roadmap Template submitted by June 10, 2026
phases.standards_addressed: Section 6 governance requirements, roadmap submission mandate

phases.title: Governance and Architecture Design
phases.description: Establish AML Solution Governance Framework covering system ownership, change control, and escalation protocols. Design integration architecture for core banking, NIBSS BVN/NIN APIs, and goAML portal. Document Alert Review SLAs and automated closure criteria. Establish Model Governance Committee for future AI/ML deployment. Draft vendor management policy and data retention policy. Conduct Business Impact Analysis with defined RTO/RPO for AML systems.
deliverables: Integration architecture design for core banking and NIBSS
Vendor management and data retention policies

phases.title: System Deployment and Integration
phases.description: Deploy RegPort for CDD/KYC, sanctions screening, transaction monitoring, and regulatory reporting. Implement real-time BVN/NIN integration via NIBSS APIs. Deploy RegGuard for fraud monitoring with feed to AML risk scoring. Implement RegComply for case management, audit trails, and dashboards. Configure transaction monitoring scenarios for retail deposits, trade finance, and ATM/POS channels. Conduct user acceptance testing and parallel run with manual processes. Migrate historical customer data and risk profiles.
deliverables: RegPort deployment with NIBSS BVN/NIN integration
RegGuard fraud monitoring with AML risk feed

phases.title: Assurance and Examination Readiness
phases.description: Conduct internal audit of AML systems including scenario effectiveness, alert quality, and governance compliance. Deploy RegLearn for staff AML training with certification tracking. Prepare CBN examination evidence pack including audit trails, case samples, STR filings, and governance documentation. Conduct scenario tuning based on alert quality metrics. Validate AI/ML models if deployed. Finalize data retention and destruction protocols. Conduct board briefing on compliance status and examination readiness.
deliverables: Internal audit report on AML systems
Staff AML training completion via RegLearn
Scenario tuning and alert quality validation

milestones.milestone: Internal audit of AML systems completed

support_section.intro_paragraph: OPEX Consulting and RegTech365 deliver end-to-end AML compliance solutions for Nigerian financial institutions. Our integrated approach combines regulatory advisory, policy development, platform deployment, and ongoing assurance. We have supported over 40 Nigerian institutions through CBN compliance programmes including the 2019 AML/CFT Regulations and the 2026 Baseline Standards.
support_section.differentiator: OPEX Consulting is the only Nigerian advisory firm offering integrated RegTech365 platform deployment with regulatory consulting. We complete CBN roadmap templates, draft board-approved policies, conduct vendor evaluations, and provide internal audit co-sourcing — ensuring seamless compliance from strategy to examination readiness.

products.name: RegPort
products.tagline: Integrated AML Platform for CBN Baseline Standards Compliance
products.description: RegPort unifies CDD/KYC, sanctions/PEP screening, transaction monitoring, customer risk assessment, and regulatory reporting in a single platform. The platform is purpose-built for Nigerian financial institutions, featuring real-time BVN/NIN integration via NIBSS, automated goAML/NFIU filing, and AI-enabled fuzzy matching for international sanctions lists.

products.name: RegGuard
products.tagline: Real-Time Fraud Monitoring with Bidirectional Risk Feed
products.description: RegGuard provides real-time fraud monitoring for card issuance, mobile money, and agent network channels. It detects pattern-based fraud like SIM swap attacks and account takeover, with an automated feed that pushes fraud alerts directly into customer risk scores to identify money laundering disguised as fraud.

products.name: RegComply
products.tagline: Enterprise Case Management with Immutable Audit Trails
products.description: RegComply delivers the mandatory Case Management and Governance Audit layer. It enforces Maker-Checker workflows for investigation, provides role-based access for MLROs, and maintains tamper-proof audit logs of all configuration changes and alert dispositions required for CBN examination.

products.name: RegLearn
products.tagline: AML Training Programme with Documented Records
products.description: RegLearn provides role-specific AML/CFT/CPF training modules covering the 12 Baseline Standards. The platform tracks staff completion, assessment scores, and certification status, producing the documented training records specifically mandated under Section 6.7 of the Circular.
